summary: Phase 2 Banquet & Event Services cart-checkout — backend 100% (8/8 pytest), frontend e2e 100% (services render, tabs, add-to-cart, qty stepper, drawer, checkout, validation, success, localStorage clear). Order EVT-000005 successfully created via UI. Customer-facing package listing did NOT surface the bundle (filtered out), noted as minor issue.

ui_bugs.issue: After clear() the cart.event_date becomes null and the 'Event date' row on the success card renders an empty value. Should snapshot event_date BEFORE clearing the cart so the confirmation displays the booked date.

integration_issues.issue: GET /api/banquets/packages/ uses get_operator_filter(current_user). For a customer (no operator_id) the filter likely scopes results away — packages-section was NOT rendered for the customer despite a valid active bundle being seeded by super_admin. Either the packages list endpoint must be public/customer-friendly (no operator scoping for customers) OR the BanquetResults page must hit a dedicated marketplace endpoint. As a result, the 'Add Bundle' one-click flow could not be exercised through the UI.

action_items: FIX: GET /api/banquets/packages/ scoping — customer role currently receives 0 packages because get_operator_filter scopes by operator_id. Make package listing public (or customer-aware) so the marketplace shows seeded bundles.
FIX (low): Capture cart.event_date before clear() in BanquetCheckout.submit() so the success card displays the date instead of an empty row.
Consider returning 422 vs the explicit 400 for missing event_date (Pydantic returns 422 for missing required field — current code only emits 400 for the parsable-but-past case).
critical_code_review_comments: _price_line for per_hour services: when hours=None on the request AND the service has no duration_hours, falls back to 1h silently — recommend rejecting with 400 ('hours required for per_hour services').

Order's top-level operator_id is set to the FIRST line-item's operator. With mixed-operator carts this single field makes operator dashboard scoping ambiguous — already noted in code comment but worth highlighting.
BanquetCheckout.submit clears the cart BEFORE the success screen renders the event_date, leading to an empty Event-date row on confirmation.
seed_data_creation: Backend test fixture creates TEST_Hall (hall, per_event 100k), TEST_Chair (rental_item, per_unit 500/chair), TEST_Photog (photographer, per_hour 30k × 4h default), and one TEST_Bundle (hall + 100 chairs, 10% off). Plus the BanquetResults pre-existing 'Cart E2E Hall' from a previous session.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend cart-checkout endpoint is fully validated by pytest (math, validation, auth, package expansion, per_hour hours-override). Frontend UI flow is fully working — services render, add-to-cart, qty stepper, drawer, checkout, validation, success screen, localStorage clear all confirmed (order EVT-000005 created end-to-end). Two outstanding issues: (1) GET /api/banquets/packages/ does not show bundles to customer role due to operator_filter scoping — package one-click flow could not be tested through UI; (2) Success screen shows blank Event-date row after clear(). The Vite dev server occasionally returns 429 on cold load — first attempt rendered 0 services, second attempt after a 30s wait rendered all 4 services correctly.
rca_of_the_issue: Packages not visible to customer: list_banquet_packages applies get_operator_filter(current_user). For customer role (no operator_id), this returns a query that doesn't match the bundle's operator_id, so the customer sees 0 packages. Fix: bypass operator_filter for customer/anonymous reads on this endpoint, or return all is_active bundles regardless of operator scope.
